perturbation.original_text: Except as provided elsewhere in this Section 4, if a Party determines in good faith that it is required under applicable law or legal process to disclose any Confidential Information of a type referred to in clauses (i), (ii), (iii) or (iv) of Section 1(a) (such Confidential Information “Technical Information”), such Party shall give the other Party prompt notice of such fact so that the other Party may obtain a protective order or any other appropriate remedy concerning any such disclosure and/or waive compliance with the nondisclosure requirements of this Agreement. The disclosing Party will reasonably cooperate with the other Party in connection with the other Party’s efforts to obtain any such order or other remedy. If any such order or other remedy does not fully preclude disclosure, or if the other Party waives such compliance, the disclosing Party may make such disclosure, to the extent that the disclosing Party determines in good faith that such disclosure is required under applicable law or legal process, and the disclosing Party will use its best efforts to have confidential treatment accorded to the disclosed Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may publicly disclose (i) Confidential Information of the type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1 (a) above and (ii) the fact that the Parties are or have been discussing a proposed Transaction and/or the terms of such proposed Transaction, in each case, only if and to the extent that the Receiving Party determines in good faith based on an opinion of legal counsel that it is required to do so under applicable law or subpoena or other legal process in connection with a Transaction, provided that the Receiving Party furnishes a copy of the proposed disclosure ORCC not less than five business days prior to such disclosure (unless the disclosing Party determines in good faith that it is not reasonably practicable to give such advance notice) and considers in good faith ORCC’s comments on such proposed disclosure. For the avoidance of doubt, this Agreement is not intended to restrict Receiving Party from making any public or private proposal or offer to acquire ORCC or its securities for all cash consideration, or from making such public disclosure in connection therewith as Receiving Party may determine in good faith based on an opinion of legal counsel to be required in accordance with the preceding sentence. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ORCC may publicly disclose the fact that the Parties are or have been discussing a proposed Transaction and/or the terms of such proposed Transaction only if and to the extent that it determines in good faith that it is required to do so under applicable law or legal process in connection with a Transaction, provided that ORCC gives the Receiving Party notice thereof three business days prior to such disclosure unless ORCC determines in good faith that it is not reasonably practicable to give such advance notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as provided elsewhere in this Section 4, if a Party determines in good faith that it is required under applicable law or legal process to disclose any Confidential Information, such Party shall give the other Party prompt notice of such fact so that the other Party may obtain a protective order or any other appropriate remedy concerning any such disclosure and/or waive compliance with the nondisclosure requirements of this Agreement. The disclosing Party will reasonably cooperate with the other Party in connection with the other Party’s efforts to obtain any such order or other remedy. If any such order or other remedy does not fully preclude disclosure, or if the other Party waives such compliance, the disclosing Party may make such disclosure, to the extent that the disclosing Party determines in good faith that such disclosure is required under applicable law or legal process, and the disclosing Party will use its best efforts to have confidential treatment accorded to the disclosed Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may publicly disclose (i) Confidential Information of the type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1 (a) above and (ii) the fact that the Parties are or have been discussing a proposed Transaction and/or the terms of such proposed Transaction, in each case, only if and to the extent that the Receiving Party determines in good faith based on an opinion of legal counsel that it is required to do so under applicable law or subpoena or other legal process in connection with a Transaction, provided that the Receiving Party furnishes a copy of the proposed disclosure ORCC not less than five business days prior to such disclosure (unless the disclosing Party determines in good faith that it is not reasonably practicable to give such advance notice) and considers in good faith ORCC’s comments on such proposed disclosure. For the avoidance of doubt, this Agreement is not intended to restrict Receiving Party from making any public or private proposal or offer to acquire ORCC or its securities for all cash consideration, or from making such public disclosure in connection therewith as Receiving Party may determine in good faith based on an opinion of legal counsel to be required in accordance with the preceding sentence. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ORCC may publicly disclose the fact that the Parties are or have been discussing a proposed Transaction and/or the terms of such proposed Transaction only if and to the extent that it determines in good faith that it is required to do so under applicable law or legal process in connection with a Transaction, provided that ORCC gives the Receiving Party notice thereof three business days prior to such disclosure unless ORCC determines in good faith that it is not reasonably practicable to give such advance notice.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "of a type referred to in clauses (i), (ii), (iii) or (iv) of Section 1(a) (such Confidential Information 'Technical Information')," from Section 4, the definition of what requires a specific action (notice, opportunity for protective order) becomes much broader. This removal is directly contradictory, because previously it was specified that only certain types of Confidential Information needed those actions. Section 5 discusses the “Term” of the Confidentiality agreement, but this perturbation now makes it unclear what information is confidential for what time period, if all information is confidential, the Term of obligations in Section 5 is contradicted because it has no specific meaning due to change in Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall control and govern all Confidential Information disclosed by ORCC to Receiving Party from and after the Effective Date (as defined above) for a period of one (1) year (the “Term”) unless earlier terminated by the Parties in a separate agreement or instrument executed by both Parties. Promptly upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or with respect to Technical Information only, upon. ORCC’s reasonable written request, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all written material (if any) containing or reflecting any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall direct its Representatives to promptly undertake the same measures described in the preceding sentence. In the event Receiving Party elects to destroy Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall provide ORCC with written certification of such destruction executed by Receiving Party’s officer who supervised such destruction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain only such copies of Confidential Information, subject to the terms and conditions hereunder, as may be necessary or required in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes, provided that counsel to Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information of a type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1(a) hereof as it determines in good faith to be necessary to advise 2 Receiving Party in connection with any disclosure permitted by Section 4 hereof provided that all such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the provisions of this Agreement for so long as such information is retained notwithstanding any time limitations set forth in this Agreement, Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information received during the Term shall survive and continue for 1 year from and after the Effective Date; provided, however, that (i) Receiving Party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall survive and continue for the longer of 1 year or as long as the same remains a trade secret under applicable law and (ii) this Section 5 will not apply to any information that Receiving Party or its Representatives has previously disclosed as permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the notice requirement in Section 4 applies only to 'Technical Information' (defined by clauses i-iv of Section 1(a)). The changed text removes this limitation, making the notice requirement apply to *any* Confidential Information. Section 5 defines the 'Term' for *all* Confidential Information disclosed by ORCC, but also contains carve-outs that relate to the 'Technical Information' definition (clauses i-iv of Section 1(a)). Removing the 'Technical Information' definition from the Section 4 notice requirement, and thus expanding the scope of section 4, creates a contradiction with Section 5, which has specific requirements dependent on that 'Technical Information' definition. Because now all information is treated the same by section 4, certain aspects of section 5 lose their meaning. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is created.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party may only disclose Confidential Information provided to it by ORCC hereunder to Receiving Party’s officers, directors, subsidiaries, affiliates, employees, legal counsel, financial advisors and other duly authorized agents and consultants (its “Representatives”) each of whom Receiving Party reasonably determines need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of assisting the Receiving Party in its evaluation of a Transaction as set forth in this Agreement; provided, however, that Receiving Party shall, prior to disclosing Confidential Information to one or more Representatives in each instance, inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and instruct them to maintain the confidentiality of such Confidential Information in a manner consistent with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party may only disclose Confidential Information provided to it by ORCC hereunder to Receiving Party’s officers, directors, subsidiaries, affiliates, employees, legal counsel, financial advisors and other duly authorized agents and consultants (its “Representatives”) each of whom Receiving Party reasonably determines need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of assisting the Receiving Party in its evaluation of a Transaction as set forth in this Agreement; provided, however, that Receiving Party shall inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "and instruct them to maintain the confidentiality of such Confidential Information in a manner consistent with the terms and conditions of this Agreement" from Section 3, Receiving Party is only required to inform representatives of its confidential nature, but not to instruct them on how to maintain the confidentiality. This removal directly contradicts Section 2 where it says "Receiving Party shall at all times protect Confidential Information, using at minimum those measures that it takes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature". If the representatives are not instructed to maintain confidentiality, it creates a contradiction because now it's not clear if the responsibility to protect Confidential Information extends to the representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ORCC will disclose certain Confidential Information to Receiving Party solely for the mutually acknowledged purpose recited above, and Receiving Party shall use such Confidential Information only for such purpose. Except to the extent expressly permitted under this Agreement, Receiving Party shall not disclose, disseminate or cause or permit any disclosure or unauthorized use of any Confidential Information, nor shall Receiving Party make any copies, reproductions, summaries or excerpts of any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall not use any Confidential Information, including, without limitation, any Technical Information, as a basis for developing, marketing or providing products or services that compete (whether directly or indirectly) with the products or services that ORCC sells, markets or provides to its customers. Receiving Party shall at all times protect Confidential Information, using at minimum those measures that it takes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature (provided that such measures are consistent with at least a reasonable degree of care); provided, however, that nothing herein shall relieve Receiving Party of its liability for unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information. In the event Receiving Party loses or causes an unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, it will notify ORCC promptly after becoming aware of such disclosure, and will use commercially reasonable efforts to secure the retrieval and return of such Confidential Information. Any delay or failure by ORCC to enforce its rights in the event of any breach of this Agreement shall not be construed to constitute a waiver or release of its rights with respect to such breach or any subsequent breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligated the Receiving Party to both inform its representatives of the confidential nature of the information and instruct them on maintaining that confidentiality consistent with the agreement. The changed text removes the instruction requirement. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states "Receiving Party shall at all times protect Confidential Information, using at minimum those measures that it takes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature". Without instructing representatives to maintain confidentiality, it's unclear if the Receiving Party is adequately protecting the information, thus creating a direct conflict as they are no longer explicitly required to ensure their representatives handle the information according to the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or with respect to Technical Information only, upon. ORCC’s reasonable written request, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all written material (if any) containing or reflecting any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall direct its Representatives to promptly undertake the same measures described in the preceding sentence. In the event Receiving Party elects to destroy Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall provide ORCC with written certification of such destruction executed by Receiving Party’s officer who supervised such destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all written material (if any) containing or reflecting any Confidential Information. In the event Receiving Party elects to destroy Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall provide ORCC with written certification of such destruction executed by Receiving Party’s officer who supervised such destruction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "or with respect to Technical Information only, upon. ORCC’s reasonable written request, Receiving Party shall direct its Representatives to promptly undertake the same measures described in the preceding sentence." from Section 5, the terms of destruction and with-holding the documentation and Confidential Information become ambiguous for representatives and are only defined for Receiving Party. This removal introduces ambiguity in Section 5, because the representatives are not mentioned in the second part of the clause. Also, this removes the option for the ORCC to ask for Technical Information at any time and the Receiving Party is not obliged to do so upon request, therefore creating a contradiction within section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall control and govern all Confidential Information disclosed by ORCC to Receiving Party from and after the Effective Date (as defined above) for a period of one (1) year (the “Term”) unless earlier terminated by the Parties in a separate agreement or instrument executed by both Parties. Promptly upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all written material (if any) containing or reflecting any Confidential Information. In the event Receiving Party elects to destroy Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall provide ORCC with written certification of such destruction executed by Receiving Party’s officer who supervised such destruction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain only such copies of Confidential Information, subject to the terms and conditions hereunder, as may be necessary or required in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes, provided that counsel to Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information of a type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1(a) hereof as it determines in good faith to be necessary to advise 2 Receiving Party in connection with any disclosure permitted by Section 4 hereof provided that all such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the provisions of this Agreement for so long as such information is retained notwithstanding any time limitations set forth in this Agreement, Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information received during the Term shall survive and continue for 1 year from and after the Effective Date; provided, however, that (i) Receiving Party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall survive and continue for the longer of 1 year or as long as the same remains a trade secret under applicable law and (ii) this Section 5 will not apply to any information that Receiving Party or its Representatives has previously disclosed as permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "or with respect to Technical Information only, upon. ORCC’s reasonable written request, Receiving Party shall direct its Representatives to promptly undertake the same measures described in the preceding sentence." introduces a contradiction. The original text explicitly allows ORCC to request Technical Information at any time and obligates the Receiving Party and their representatives to comply. By removing this, the changed text now lacks this provision, creating a direct conflict with the Contradicted Text and the agreement as a whole, as now the ORCC may not be able to request technical information during the agreement. This falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction as defined.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain only such copies of Confidential Information, subject to the terms and conditions hereunder, as may be necessary or required in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes, provided that counsel to Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information of a type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1(a) hereof as it determines in good faith to be necessary to advise 2 Receiving Party in connection with any disclosure permitted by Section 4 hereof provided that all such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the provisions of this Agreement for so long as such information is retained notwithstanding any time limitations set forth in this Agreement, Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information received during the Term shall survive and continue for 1 year from and after the Effective Date; provided, however, that (i) Receiving Party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall survive and continue for the longer of 1 year or as long as the same remains a trade secret under applicable law and (ii) this Section 5 will not apply to any information that Receiving Party or its Representatives has previously disclosed as permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain only such copies of Confidential Information, subject to the terms and conditions hereunder, as may be necessary or required in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes, provided that counsel to Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information as it determines in good faith to be necessary to advise 2 Receiving Party in connection with any disclosure permitted by Section 4 hereof provided that all such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the provisions of this Agreement for so long as such information is retained notwithstanding any time limitations set forth in this Agreement, Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information received during the Term shall survive and continue for 1 year from and after the Effective Date; provided, however, that (i) Receiving Party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall survive and continue for the longer of 1 year or as long as the same remains a trade secret under applicable law and (ii) this Section 5 will not apply to any information that Receiving Party or its Representatives has previously disclosed as permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "of a type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1(a) hereof" from Section 5, allowing counsel to retain copies of *any* Confidential Information, the provision becomes overly broad. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the sentence, where the retention of Confidential Information is restricted to Receiving Party's 'internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes.' Now, counsel can retain any Confidential Information they deem necessary, regardless of the defined purposes, hence creating a contradiction of how long and why that information is being retained.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall control and govern all Confidential Information disclosed by ORCC to Receiving Party from and after the Effective Date (as defined above) for a period of one (1) year (the “Term”) unless earlier terminated by the Parties in a separate agreement or instrument executed by both Parties. Promptly upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or with respect to Technical Information only, upon. ORCC’s reasonable written request, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all written material (if any) containing or reflecting any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall direct its Representatives to promptly undertake the same measures described in the preceding sentence. In the event Receiving Party elects to destroy Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall provide ORCC with written certification of such destruction executed by Receiving Party’s officer who supervised such destruction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain only such copies of Confidential Information, subject to the terms and conditions hereunder, as may be necessary or required in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes, provided that counsel to Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information as it determines in good faith to be necessary to advise 2 Receiving Party in connection with any disclosure permitted by Section 4 hereof provided that all such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the provisions of this Agreement for so long as such information is retained notwithstanding any time limitations set forth in this Agreement, Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information received during the Term shall survive and continue for 1 year from and after the Effective Date; provided, however, that (i) Receiving Party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall survive and continue for the longer of 1 year or as long as the same remains a trade secret under applicable law and (ii) this Section 5 will not apply to any information that Receiving Party or its Representatives has previously disclosed as permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited counsel's retention of confidential information to a specific type (clause (iv) of Section 1(a)). The changed text removes this limitation, allowing counsel to retain any confidential information they deem necessary, regardless of the initial restriction. This creates a contradiction with the earlier part of the same section of the contradicted text, which states that the Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information only as necessary in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes. Now counsel can retain any confidential information, and how long it is retained, which contradicts that requirement.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall not reverse-engineer, decompile or disassemble any software disclosed under this Agreement and shall not remove, overprint or change any notice, including as to ownership or confidentiality from any originals or copies of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall not reverse-engineer, decompile or disassemble any software disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "and shall not remove, overprint or change any notice, including as to ownership or confidentiality from any originals or copies of Confidential Information." from Section 6, it creates a contradiction with Section 6, because in the first part of the sentence, that obligation is no longer in the best interests for the business and confidential agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ORCC owns and shall retain all right, title and interest in and to all Confidential Information, regardless of disclosure hereunder. Absolutely no present or future patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret right or other intellectual property right, nor any right, license, title or interest of any kind, is given, granted, transferred or otherwise conveyed by or under this Agreement with respect to any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall not reverse-engineer, decompile or disassemble any software disclosed under this Agreement and shall not remove, overprint or change any notice, including as to ownership or confidentiality from any originals or copies of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6 included a provision that the Receiving Party shall not remove, overprint, or change any notice regarding ownership or confidentiality. The changed text removes this provision. The contradicted text, also found in Section 6, states that ORCC retains all rights to Confidential Information and that the Receiving Party shall not remove or change any notice regarding ownership or confidentiality. By removing the Receiving Party's obligation not to remove or change notices, the modified text directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly prohibits such actions.
